searchDocs.title: Exception​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Exception { message: string formatted: boolean outputFn: <T...>(T...) → () }

searchDocs.title: Throw​
searchDocs.content: </> Exceptions.Throw( e: Exception, origin: String, ...: any-- Optional arguments if an exception is formatted ) → () Throws a given exception with an optional 'origin' parameter to help debug. local e = require("@path/to/Exceptions") type Exception = e.Exception local sampleException: Exception = e.new("Sample exception has occurred.", warn) local var: Number = 42 if (var ~= 50) then e.Throw(sampleException, "LOCATION") --Warns "[LOCATION]: Sample exception has occurred." end

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Exceptions.new( exceptionMsg: string,-- Message to display when the exception is thrown. isFormatted: boolean,-- If the message should be formatted when the exception is thrown. outputFn: <T...>(T...) → ()-- The function to call when the exception is thrown. ) → Exception Constructs and returns a new 'Exception' with the given parameters. local e = require("@path/to/Exceptions") type Exception = e.Exception local sampleException: Exception = e.new("Sample exception has occurred.", warn)

searchDocs.title: InterLink
searchDocs.content: InterLink A small component framework providing identity, tags, attributes, events, and deterministic lifecycles for Instance hierarchies. Designed to be lightweight and robust, InterLink aims to reducde bloat and streamline development. Example Usage: --// DEPENDENCIES local Interlink = require(game.ReplicatedStorage.Modules.InterLink) --// TYPE DECLARATIONS type Root<T> = InterLink.Root<T> type Component<T> = InterLink.Component<T> --// LOCAL VARIABLES local randomFolder: Folder = game.Workspace:WaitForChild("RandomFolder") --// INTERLINK local Root: Root<Folder> = assert(Interlink.Root.new(randomFolder), "'Root' cannot be nil.") local testObj1: Component<Part> = assert(Root:GetById("testObj1"), "'testObj1' cannot be nil.") --// CONNECTION FUNCTIONS local function partTouched() print("testObj1 was touched!") end local function testEvent(...) print(...) end --// CONNECTIONS testObj1.Touched:Connect(partTouched) -- Anything referenced in a component that is not a valid property of -- the component or its instance will automatically be turned into a SignaLuau signal. testObj1.testEvent:Connect(testEvent) --// MISC testObj1.testEvent:Fire("Hello, world!")

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Root.new(root: Instance ) → Root<T>

interface Root<T> { Instance: T Components: {[string]: Component<any>} Tags: {[string]: {[string]: boolean}} Attributes: {[string]: {[string]: {[string]: boolean}}} GetById: <U>( self: Root<T>, queryId: string ) → (Component<U>?), GetByTag: ( self: Root<T>, queryTag: string ) → ({Component<any>}), GetByAttribute: ( self: Root<T>, queryAttribute: string, value: any ) → ({Component<any>}), Destroy: (self: Root<T>) → () }  Returns a constructed 'Root' object with generic type 'T'.

searchDocs.title: GetById​
searchDocs.content: </> Root:GetById(queryId: string) → Component<T> Returns the component with the specified id if found, otherwise returning nil.

searchDocs.title: GetByTag​
searchDocs.content: </> Root:GetByTag(queryTag: string) → {Component<any>} Returns a table of components with the specified tag if found, otherwise returning {}.

searchDocs.title: GetByAttribute​
searchDocs.content: </> Root:GetByAttribute( queryAttribute: string, value: any ) → {Component<any>} Returns a table of components with the specified attribute and specified value if found, otherwise returning {}.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Root:Destroy() → () Destroys the root, which includes destroying any related components as well as the instance it wraps.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Component.new( _Id: string, _Instance: Instance ) → Component<T>

interface Component<T> { Id: string Instance: T Tags: {string} Attributes: {[string]: any} Events: {[string]: {Signal<...any>}} Destroy: (self: Component<T>) → () }  Returns a constructed 'Component' object with generic type .

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Component:Destroy() → () Destroys the component, which includes destroying any related signals as well as the instance it wraps.

searchDocs.title: SignaLuau
searchDocs.content: SignaLuau A lightweight, consumer-grade Luau module for implementing custom signals and signal connections, designed to closely mimic the built-in RBXScriptSignal and RBXScriptConnection behavior in Roblox.

searchDocs.title: Getting Started
searchDocs.content: Getting Started This will be implemented at a later date

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> SignalConnection.new(disconnectFunction: Function) → SignalConnection
interface SignalConnection { Connected: boolean Disconnect: (SignalConnection) → () }  Returns a constructed Signal object with the proper disconnectFunction.

searchDocs.title: Disconnect​
searchDocs.content: </> SignalConnection:Disconnect() → () Disconnects a SignalConnection from the signal.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal.new() → Signal<T...>

interface Signal<T...> { Connect: ( Signal<T...>, (...T) → () ) → (SignalConnection) Destroy: (Signal<T...>) → () Fire: ( Signal<T...>, ...T ) → () FireAsync: ( Signal<T...>, ...T ) → () Once: ( Signal<T...>, (...T) → () ) → (SignalConnection) Wait: (Signal<T...>) → (...T) }  Returns a constructed Signal object local Signal = require("@path/toSignaLuau") type Signal<T...> = Signal.Signal<T...> local signal: Signal<string> = Signal.new()

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Destroy() → () Cleans up Signal object by disconnecting all connections. signal:Destroy()

searchDocs.title: Connect​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Connect(callback: (...any) → ()) → SignalConnection? Connects a given function to the Signal object. Will run until :Disconnect() is called, or the connection's signal is destroyed. signal:Connect(function(msg: string) print(msg) end) signal:Fire("Message 1")

searchDocs.title: Once​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Once(callback: (...any) → ()) → SignalConnection? Connects a given function to the Signal object. Connection remains open until it is first fired, then cleans itself up. signal:Once(function(msg: string) print(msg) end) signal:Fire("Message 1") signal:Fire("Message 1") -- Will not print

searchDocs.title: Wait​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Signal:Wait() → ...any Yields the current thread until the event is fired, returning the results and resuming the thread. task.spawn(function() local msg = signal:Wait() print(msg) end) task.wait(3) signal:Fire("Hello, world!") -- Prints output 3 seconds after script is executed

searchDocs.title: Fire​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Fire(...: any) → () Fires a signal, calling all connected functions associated with the Signal with the given arguments. signal:Fire("Hello, world!", 100, false)

searchDocs.title: FireAsync​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:FireAsync(...: any) → () Fires a signal, calling all connected functions associated with the Signal with the given arguments in a new thread. signal:FireAsync("Hello, world!", 100, false)
